# mf-app-fintech-rewardprogram

React Native microfrontend for Reward Program. Consumed by SuperApp.

## Quick Start

1. Read `progress.txt` for current tasks and detailed implementation notes
2. Read `STYLE_GUIDE.md` for Zustand + Hook Component patterns
3. Run `npm run typecheck`

## Architecture

| Aspect  | Technology                                             |
| ------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| Type    | React Native microfrontend (exports raw TypeScript)    |
| State   | Zustand (new domains) + Redux (legacy, being migrated) |
| Pattern | Hook Component (`index.ts` logic + `view.tsx` render)  |

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── services/          # Domain logic (Zustand - SOLID architecture)
│   ├── api/           # HTTP clients
│   ├── models/        # Types, interfaces, enums
│   ├── repository/    # Data mapping
│   ├── services/      # Business logic
│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices
│   ├── hooks/         # Service hooks
│   └── utils/         # Helpers (cleanup, participantId)
├── screens/           # 10 screens (Hook Component pattern)
├── atomic/            # UI components (atoms, organisms, templates)
├── redux/             # Legacy state (being migrated to Zustand)
│   └── ducks/         # points.ts, rescue.ts, getMarketplace.ts
├── routes/            # Navigation
├── hooks/             # General hooks
└── index.ts           # Exports
```

## Zustand Domains

| Domain       | Status   | Layers                                       |
| ------------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| airlineMiles | Complete | api, model, service, store, hook             |
| extract      | Complete | api, model, repository, service, store       |
| parameter    | Complete | api, model, repository, service, store       |
| permission   | Complete | api, model, repository, service, store, hook |
| products     | Pending  | needs all layers                             |
| rescue       | Pending  | needs all layers                             |
| marketplace  | Pending  | needs all layers                             |

## The `~` Alias (Critical)

All `~/` imports resolve to **SuperApp's code** at runtime:

- `~/store` → SuperApp's Zustand store (MANDATORY for store imports)
- `~/redux/*` → Legacy Redux
- `~/routes/*` → Navigation

**Tests only**: `~` maps to `./src/` via babel module-resolver.

## Code Rules

1. Store imports **MUST** use `~/store` (never relative paths)
2. Screens **MUST** follow Hook Component pattern
3. **NO LOGIC CHANGES** during migration - structure only
4. Verify with `npm run typecheck`
5. Never `git push`, only commit

## Commands

| Command                | Purpose                      |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| `npm run typecheck`    | validations                  |
| `npm run sync-typings` | Generate types from SuperApp |
| `yarn sync:yalc`       | Push to SuperApp for testing |
| `npm test`             | Run tests                    |

## Exports (src/index.ts)

```typescript
export { RewardsProgramRoutes } from './routes';
export { type RewardsProgramRootStore } from './services';
export { setRewardsProgramSlices } from './services/store/slices';
export { clearRewardsProgramStore } from './services/utils';
```

## Current Work

See `progress.txt` for:

- Detailed migration tasks (41 subtasks)
- Redux state shapes to migrate
- Zustand patterns to follow
- Test patterns
- Complete code snippets

## Documentation

| File             | Purpose                                          |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `CLAUDE.md`      | This file - quick reference                      |
| `progress.txt`   | Detailed tasks, code snippets, persistent memory |
| `STYLE_GUIDE.md` | Zustand + Hook Component patterns (root folder)  |

# App Sem Parar

[![Build Status](https://azuredevops.fleetcor.com.br/Fleetcor/TPS/_apis/build/status/TPS/MobileApp/appSemParar?branchName=develop)](https://azuredevops.fleetcor.com.br/Fleetcor/TPS/_build/latest?definitionId=1911&branchName=develop)

Documentação com passo a passo para configurar o ambiente e executar o aplicativo.

## 💻 Configuração de ambiente

### Windows

- [Node](https://nodejs.org/pt-br/download/)
- [JDK](https://www.oracle.com/br/java/technologies/javase/javase-jdk8-downloads.html)
- [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)

### macOS

- [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- [Xcode](https://apps.apple.com/br/app/xcode/id497799835)

Abra o terminal e digite o comando abaixo para instalar o homebrew

```bash
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
```

Instalação do node

```bash
brew install node
```

Instalação do watchman

```bash
brew install watchman
```

Instalação do JDK

```bash
brew install --cask adoptopenjdk/openjdk/adoptopenjdk8
```

#### Android

Adicione as seguintes variáveis no seu arquivo **.bash_profile**

```javascript
export ANDROID_HOME=$HOME/Library/Android/sdk
export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/emulator
export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/tools
export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/tools/bin
export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools
```

Caso não possua o arquivo, execute os comandos:

```bash
touch .bash_profile && open .bash_profile
```

Para os macs com chip ARM (Apple M1, por exemplo) utilize [este emulador aqui](https://github.com/google/android-emulator-m1-preview/releases/tag/0.3) ou configure um ARM64 no **AVD Manager** > **Outras Imagens**

#### IOS

Para IOS o processo é diferente dependendo da arquitetura do chip do Mac.

#### Mac Intel

Execute o comando:

```bash
sudo gem install cocoapods
```

Entre na pasta do projeto e execute o comando:

```bash
cd ios && pod install && pod update
```

#### Mac ARM

No caso dos macs com chip ARM (Apple M1, por exemplo) é preciso executar os comandos no terminal rosetta, segue um passo a passo simples para a criação do terminal:

- Clique no **Finder** e vá até **Aplicativos > Utilitários**
- Clique com o botão direito no **Terminal** e em **Duplicar**
- Renomeie o terminal duplicado (Rosetta terminal, por exemplo)
- Clique com o botão direito no novo terminal e em **Obter Informações**
- Marque o checkbox **Abrir com Rosetta**

No novo terminal criado execute os comandos:

```bash
sudo gem install ffi && sudo gem install cocoapods
```

Entre na pasta do projeto e execute o comando:

```bash
cd ios && pod install && pod update
```



## 🚀 Executando o projeto

Abra o terminal, navegue até a pasta do projeto e execute os seguintes comandos:

### Windows

```bash
  reat-native run-android 
```


### macOS


```bash
  reat-native run-ios 
```

Ou


```bash
  reat-native run-android 
```
 
Em caso de erro na execução do android:

```bash
cd android &&./gradlew clean
```


## Links úteis

 - [Documentação React Native](https://reactnative.dev/docs/getting-started)
 - [Documentação Android](https://developer.android.com/docs)
 - [Documentação Xcode](https://developer.apple.com/pt/support/xcode/)
 - [Apple developer forum M1 error](https://developer.apple.com/forums/thread/670006)

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→
    12→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Introduction
    28→
    29→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30→
    31→Key decisions:
    32→
    33→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.

    38→---
    39→
    40→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    41→
    42→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43→
    44→```
    45→screens/Home/
    46→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48→└── Models/
    49→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Models/index.d.ts**
    53→
    54→```ts
    55→export interface HomeParams {
    56→  welcome?: string;
    57→}
    58→export interface HomeState {
    59→  greeting: string;
    60→  isLoading: boolean;
    61→}
    62→export interface HomeHandlers {
    63→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64→}
    65→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66→```
    67→
    68→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69→
    70→```ts
    71→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73→import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74→
    75→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76→  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78→
    79→  useEffect(() => {
    80→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82→  }, []);
    83→
    84→  const greeting = useMemo(
    85→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86→    [
    87→      params?.welcome,
    88→      name,
    89→    ],
    90→  );
    91→
    92→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94→  }, []);
    95→
    96→  return {
    97→    greeting,
    98→    isLoading,
    99→    onPressCTA,
   100→  };
   101→}
   102→```

   149→```ts
   150→export interface ButtonProps {

   152→  disabled?: boolean;
   153→  onPress?: () => void;
   154→}
   155→export interface ButtonState {

   164→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   165→
   166→```ts
   167→import { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
   168→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from './Models';
   169→
   170→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   171→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   172→
   173→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   174→    setPressed(true);
   175→    props.onPress?.();
   176→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   177→  }, [props.onPress]);
   178→
   179→  return {
   180→    pressed,
   181→    handlePress,
   182→  };
   183→}
   184→```

   244→---
   245→
   246→## Services Architecture
   247→
   248→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   249→
   250→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   251→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   252→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   253→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   254→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   255→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   256→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   257→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   258→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   259→
   260→Principles:
   261→
   262→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   263→- No circular dependencies.
   264→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   265→
   266→---
   267→
   268→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   269→
   270→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   271→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   272→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   273→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   274→
   275→**Slice example**
   276→
   277→```ts
   278→import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
   279→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from '../models/store';
   280→
   281→export type AuthSlice = {
   282→  auth: AuthState;
   283→  authActions: AuthActions;
   284→};
   285→
   286→const initialAuth = {
   287→  user: null,
   288→  loading: false,
   289→  error: undefined,
   290→};
   291→
   292→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<
   293→  AuthSlice,
   294→  [],
   295→  [],
   296→  AuthSlice
   297→> = set => ({
   298→  auth: initialAuth,
   299→  authActions: {
   300→    setLoading: v =>
   301→      set(s => ({
   302→        auth: {
   303→          ...s.auth,
   304→          isLoading: v,
   305→        },
   306→      })),
   307→    setUser: v =>
   308→      set(s => ({
   309→        auth: {
   310→          ...s.auth,
   311→          user: v,
   312→        },
   313→      })),
   314→    setError: v =>
   315→      set(s => ({
   316→        auth: {
   317→          ...s.auth,
   318→          error: v,
   319→        },
   320→      })),
   321→    reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   322→  },
   323→});
   324→```

   326→**Root Store**
   327→
   328→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   329→
   330→```ts
   331→import { create } from 'zustand';
   332→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
   333→import { immer } from 'zustand/middleware/immer';
   334→
   335→import {
   336→  createUserSlice,
   337→  type UserSlice,
   338→} from '@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user';
   339→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from '@semparar/mf-app-yourmf';
   340→
   341→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   342→
   343→/** Add your slice Type */
   344→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   345→
   346→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   347→  devtools(
   348→    persist(
   349→      immer((...a) => ({
   350→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   351→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   352→      })),
   353→      {
   354→        name: 'root-storage',
   355→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   356→        partialize: s => ({
   357→          user: s.user,
   358→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   359→        }),
   360→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({
   361→          ...current,
   362→          ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>),
   363→        }),
   364→      },
   365→    ),
   366→    { name: 'RootStore' },
   367→  ),
   368→);
   369→```

   508→
   509→## Cache Pattern
   510→
   511→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   512→
   513→```ts
   514→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   515→
   516→export interface CachedData<T> {
   517→  timestamp: number;
   518→  data: T;
   519→}
   520→
   521→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   522→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   523→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   524→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   525→}
   526→
   527→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   528→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   529→
   530→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   531→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   532→    if (!json) return null;
   533→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   534→  }
   535→
   536→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   537→    const payload: CachedData<T> = {
   538→      timestamp: Date.now(),
   539→      data,
   540→    };
   541→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   542→  }
   543→
   544→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   545→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   546→  }
   547→}
   548→```
   549→
   550→**Guidelines**
   551→
   552→- One storage class per module/feature.
   553→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   554→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   555→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   556→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.

   155→import { useCallback } from 'react';
   156→import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
   157→import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
   158→import {
   159→  getPointsProducts,
   160→  clearPointsProducts,
   161→  getPointsBalance,
   162→  getParameterPointsProgram,
   163→} from '../services';
   164→import {
   165→  PointsProductsStatus,
   166→  PointsBalanceStatus,
   167→  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
   168→} from '../models';
   169→
   170→export function usePoints() {
   171→  // Products from products store
   172→  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
   173→  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();
   174→
   175→  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
   176→  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));
   177→
   178→  // Parameterization from parameter store
   179→  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
   180→    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
   181→  );
   182→
   183→  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
   184→    await Promise.all([
   185→      getPointsBalance(),
   186→      getPointsProducts(),
   187→      getParameterPointsProgram(),
   188→    ]);
   189→  }, []);
   190→
   191→  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
   192→    await getPointsBalance();
   193→  }, []);
   194→
   195→  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
   196→    await getPointsProducts();
   197→  }, []);
   198→
   199→  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
   200→    await getParameterPointsProgram();
   201→  }, []);
   202→
   203→  const clear = useCallback(() => {
   204→    clearPointsProducts();
   205→  }, []);
   206→
   207→  return {
   208→    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
   209→    points: pointsBalance.points,
   210→    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
   211→    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,
   212→
   213→    // Products (from products store)
   214→    products: pointsProducts.products,
   215→    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
   216→    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,
   217→
   218→    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
   219→    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
   220→    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
   221→    isLoadingParameterization:
   222→      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
   223→    isErrorParameterization:
   224→      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
   225→
   226→    // Actions
   227→    requestPointsBalance,
   228→    requestPointsProducts,
   229→    requestParameterization,
   230→    requestInitialData,
   231→    clear,
   232→  };
   233→}

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import {
  getPointsProducts,
  clearPointsProducts,
  getPointsBalance,
  getParameterPointsProgram,
} from '../services';
import {
  PointsProductsStatus,
  PointsBalanceStatus,
  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
} from '../models';

export function usePoints() {
  // Products from products store
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));

  // Parameterization from parameter store
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
  );

  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
    await Promise.all([
      getPointsBalance(),
      getPointsProducts(),
      getParameterPointsProgram(),
    ]);
  }, []);

  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsBalance();
  }, []);

  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
    await getParameterPointsProgram();
  }, []);

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return {
    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
    points: pointsBalance.points,
    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,

    // Products (from products store)
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
    isLoadingParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsBalance,
    requestPointsProducts,
    requestParameterization,
    requestInitialData,
    clear,
  };
}
```

import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

# Claude Code Hallucinations Analysis - Redux to Zustand Migration

**Date:** 2026-01-29
**Analyzed by:** Claude Opus 4.5
**Comparison:** `rnm-app-fintech-card-place` (original) vs `rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram` (migrated)

---

## Executive Summary

During the migration from the original `card-place` repository to `rewardprogram`, several critical logic changes were introduced that violate the **"NO LOGIC CHANGES"** rule. This document identifies each hallucination with file references, impact analysis, and remediation steps.

---

## CRITICAL HALLUCINATIONS (Logic Changes)

### 1. `rescueDescription` Export Removed

**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Type:** Logic Change - Business Logic Removed

**Original (`card-place`):**
```
File: src/screens/Points/data/index.ts:74-82

export const rescueDescription: Record<string, string> = {
  "203": "Cashback na fatura",
  "204": "Desconto em Mensalidade",
  "205": "Voucher para estacionamento",
  "206": "Voucher para abastecimento",
  "207": "Voucher para Seguro Auto",
  "208": "Voucher para IPVA e outros débitos",
  "209": "Milhas Aéreas",
};
```

**Migrated (`rewardprogram`):**
```
File: src/screens/Points/data/index.ts

MISSING - Export completely removed!
```

**Impact:**
```
Original InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:112:
  description: rescueDescription[productCode]

Migrated InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:125:
  description: 'Cashback resgatado'
```

The rescue payload now sends a hardcoded `'Cashback resgatado'` string instead of the correct dynamic description based on `productCode`. This affects:
- API payload data integrity
- Backend processing (if it depends on description)
- Analytics tracking

**Remediation:**
1. Add `rescueDescription` export back to `src/screens/Points/data/index.ts`
2. Update `src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx` to import and use `rescueDescription[productCode]`

---

### 2. `productCode` Default Value Changed

**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Type:** Logic Change - Null Safety Removed

**Original (`card-place`):**
```typescript
// InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:103
const productCode = route?.params?.productCode ?? '0';
```

**Migrated (`rewardprogram`):**
```typescript
// InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:116
const productCode = route?.params?.productCode;
```

**Impact:**
- `productCode` can now be `undefined` instead of defaulting to `'0'`
- This breaks the `rescueDescription[productCode]` lookup (returns `undefined`)
- API payload may contain `undefined` productCode

**Remediation:**
1. Restore default value: `const productCode = route?.params?.productCode ?? '0';`

---

### 3. `POINTS_OPTIONS` Export Removed

**Severity:** LOW
**Type:** Export Removed

**Original (`card-place`):**
```typescript
// src/screens/Points/data/index.ts:62-72
export const POINTS_OPTIONS = [
  { name: 'Resgate de cashback em fatura' },
  { name: 'Marketplace' },
  { name: 'Voucher para abastecimento' },
  { name: 'Desconto na mensalidade' },
  { name: 'Desconto seguro auto' },
  { name: 'Desconto em estacionamento' },
  { name: 'Desconto em multas e débitos' },
  { name: 'Desconto IPVA' },
  { name: 'Milhas aéreas' },
];
```

**Migrated (`rewardprogram`):**
```
MISSING - Export completely removed!
```

**Impact:**
- Not imported anywhere in the codebase
- Low risk but should be restored for consistency

**Remediation:**
1. Add `POINTS_OPTIONS` export back to `src/screens/Points/data/index.ts`

---

### 4. `NAME_TO_KEY` Export Visibility Changed

**Severity:** LOW
**Type:** Export Visibility Change

**Original (`card-place`):**
```typescript
// src/screens/Points/data/index.ts:25
export const NAME_TO_KEY: Record<string, string> = { ... }
```

**Migrated (`rewardprogram`):**
```typescript
// src/screens/Points/data/index.ts:25
const NAME_TO_KEY: Record<string, string> = { ... }
```

**Impact:**
- Only used internally in the same file
- No external imports found
- Low risk, but inconsistent with original

**Remediation:**
1. Restore `export` keyword: `export const NAME_TO_KEY`

---

## PRE-EXISTING ISSUES (Not Migration Bugs)

These issues were documented in `progress.txt` as "PRE-EXISTING" and are confirmed to exist in the original `newArch` code in `card-place`:

### 5. `pointsQuantity` Always 0

**Status:** PRE-EXISTING (confirmed)
**Location:** `parameter.repository.ts`, `parameter.service.ts`

**Original Redux Saga (`card-place/src/redux/sagas/points/parameterization.ts`):**
```typescript
function extractPointsQuantity(parameterization: ParametrizationData[]): number {
  const pointsRescueItem = parameterization?.find(
    item => item.category === 'pointsRescueSentence'
  );
  // ... parses JSON to extract pointsQuantity
